name: Web Curios
fitEvidence: The editor explicitly invites things the sender made and curates strange, funny, creative web projects.
hardGate: Utility must be polished, public, no-signup, and genuinely fun; disclose ownership and send one personalized note.
notes: Best first editorial target for the utility, not the generic affiliate homepage.

name: The Awesomer
fitEvidence: The publication covers interesting, funky, goofy products and accepts reader suggestions through its form.
hardGate: Index needs original findings and visuals; disclose that goose.gifts is owned and affiliate-supported.
notes: Lead with one surprising finding and the methodology, not a gift-guide pitch.

name: Boing Boing
fitEvidence: The publication covers weird internet phenomena, design, makers, playful links, and explicitly accepts editorial tips.
hardGate: Only pitch an original defensible story or exceptional utility; disclose ownership and commercial interest.
notes: Second-wave editorial pitch after the Index has quote-ready evidence.

name: X
fitEvidence: Visual jokes, original findings, and a one-click utility can be native entertainment rather than repeated link posts.
hardGate: Confirm account/API access; no duplicate cross-posts, unsolicited automated replies or DMs, automated likes, or misleading redirects.
notes: Test distinct recurring formats only after a creative unit earns attention without the link.

name: Product Hunt
fitEvidence: The utility could be launched as an interactive maker product and collect structured early-user feedback.
hardGate: Utility must be substantive; account creation requires approval; never ask for upvotes, mass-message, incentivize, or coordinate voting.
notes: Traffic fit is weaker than editorial-curiosity outlets; use for feedback, not backlink theater.

name: Bluesky
fitEvidence: Independent-web and oddity communities may respond to conversational build notes and original visuals.
hardGate: Confirm account access; disclose commercial ownership; no spam, repetitive posts, manipulation, or copy-paste X feed.
notes: Write for conversation and indie-web curiosity; do not mirror X verbatim.

name: Hacker News / Show HN
fitEvidence: A technically non-trivial no-signup utility can be tried directly and discussed by its maker.
hardGate: Must be personally built, non-trivial, and immediately usable; lists, reading material, signup pages, and quick one-offs do not qualify; never solicit votes/comments.
notes: Conditional feedback channel, not a priority traffic channel.

name: Dense Discovery
fitEvidence: The publication features thoughtful independent tools, design, culture, and offbeat web discoveries.
hardGate: Confirm a current unpaid editorial-suggestion route. Paid sponsor/classified slots require separate spend authorization.
notes: Do not use an email found only in old archive issues.

name: Flipboard Publisher
fitEvidence: Approved publishers can receive referral traffic from original article distribution.
hardGate: Requires regular original content, bylines, contact information, verifiable presence, qualifying RSS, account creation, and feed review.
notes: Longer-term distribution system after several original Index/editorial editions, not a one-page shortcut.

name: Source of Sources
fitEvidence: Relevant journalist queries could use first-party gifting or unusual-product data and yield legitimate citations.
hardGate: Subscription requires approval; respond only to an exact relevant query with timely first-party evidence, never a sales pitch.
notes: Keep as a monitoring lane only after the Index establishes real evidence.

name: Qwoted
fitEvidence: A vetted source can answer relevant media requests when goose.gifts has first-party evidence.
hardGate: Account creation and vetting require approval; answer only relevant requests and do not treat the service as a backlink submission queue.
notes: Lower priority than direct pitches and SOS until an exact query proves fit.

name: Reddit communities
fitEvidence: A genuinely useful/funny utility could fit a specific community when shared by a real participant under that community's rules.
hardGate: Identify one exact community, read its current rules, confirm genuine participation history, disclose ownership, and never cross-post, automate, disguise links, or use votes/DMs.
notes: No generic Reddit launch and no draft link post until community-specific evidence exists.

name: Cool Tools
fitEvidence: The publication accepts reader reviews of useful websites and explicitly asks about creator involvement.
hardGate: Requires at least six months of personal use and transparent creator/distributor involvement.
notes: Do not submit at launch; reconsider only after sustained real use and a truthful first-person review.

name: Laughing Squid
fitEvidence: Topical tone appears superficially relevant, but the explicit submission rule controls.
hardGate: The current submission page says not to submit promotions related to products or services.
notes: Recorded to prevent future agents from repeatedly rediscovering and pitching an ineligible outlet.
